_doc: The 21 CFR Part 312 Subpart D sponsor-obligation menu for the Transfer of Regulatory Obligations (TORO, 21 CFR 312.52). 'transferable' rows may be assigned to a CRO in writing; 'non_transferable' rows never can. Citations transcribed from the vetted TORO template (templates/contracts/transfer-of-regulatory-obligations-toro.md) and the micro-cro wiki page. Nothing here is legal advice; a generated TORO is a DRAFT for qualified human review and signature.

transferable.description: Selecting qualified investigators and providing them the information they need to conduct the investigation (including the Investigator's Brochure)
Obtaining Form FDA 1572 and investigator qualification documents before permitting an investigator to begin
Selecting qualified monitors and ensuring proper monitoring of the investigation
Ensuring the investigation is conducted in accordance with the general investigational plan and protocols contained in the IND
Review of ongoing investigations; securing compliance, or discontinuing shipments and ending an investigator's participation for noncompliance
Safety review of all information relevant to the drug; IND safety reports to FDA and all participating investigators (15 calendar day / 7 calendar day)
transferable.requires_qualified_human: The seriousness / causality / expectedness determination inside this obligation is a qualified-physician (medical monitor) judgment under 21 CFR 312.32 and ICH E2A. A CRO may assume the obligation only into the hands of a qualified physician, never into software; the engine computes deadlines and escalates, it never makes the causality call.

transferable.description: Investigational new drug maintenance: preparing and submitting protocol amendments and information amendments
Preparing and submitting IND annual reports
Sponsor control and disposition of the investigational drug: shipment only to participating investigators, and disposition/return of unused supply, with accountability records
transferable.note: The investigator's own control of the drug at the site (21 CFR 312.61) is a separate, non-transferable investigator obligation.

transferable.description: Collecting and reporting financial disclosure information for clinical investigators
Permitting FDA to inspect and copy sponsor records and reports

non_transferable.description: Investigator conduct obligations: protocol adherence, control of the investigational drug at the site, maintaining case histories, reporting SAEs to the sponsor, and IRB assurance
non_transferable.reason: These bind the licensed investigator personally and are non-transferable to any vendor or entity. In a sponsor-investigator study the individual holds them as the investigator, not the sponsor, so 312.52 cannot reach them.
Obtained under the investigator's responsibility at the site; a Part 50 human act the software never performs.

non_transferable.description: The IRB / ethics determination on the research
non_transferable.reason: An independent constituted board's judgment; it is not a sponsor obligation and cannot be transferred.

non_transferable.description: The sponsor-investigator's own attestations: the Form FDA 1571 and Form FDA 1572 signatures and the Part 54 financial certification
non_transferable.reason: Personal legal acts of the signer; a signature is not an obligation that can be delegated.

non_transferable.description: The manufacturer's decision to supply the investigational drug
non_transferable.reason: The manufacturer's alone; the software models it as a request, never an action it takes.
